concerns.issue: particleField uses createPRNG(seed) without suffix, but plan uses createPRNG(seed + ':pointcloud'). This is fine for differentiation between systems but note the deviation from existing pattern.
concerns.suggestion: Keep the suffix — it prevents the two systems from generating identical initial distributions when given the same session seed.

concerns.issue: Rotating the Points mesh around Y gives parallax, but only around one axis. True depth perception benefits from multi-axis drift.
concerns.suggestion: The plan already mentions Z-axis oscillation which helps. Acceptable as-is; can iterate later.

concerns.issue: Plan references quality.enableSparkle for PointCloudConfig — enableSparkle already exists on QualityProfile so no new field needed there.
concerns.suggestion: Confirmed correct. enableSparkle is already on QualityProfile with tier values: low=false, medium=true, high=true.

concerns.issue: Test description is slightly ambiguous: 'No point has Z === 0 for all points'. Intent is clear (the cloud is not coplanar) but wording could be misread.
concerns.suggestion: Implement as: verify that not all Z values are zero, i.e. at least some points have non-zero Z. The Z-range check in T-031-03 already covers the stronger condition.

notes: GeometrySystem interface confirmed: { init(scene, seed, params), draw(scene, frame), cleanup?() }
QualityProfile already has enableSparkle — no new field needed for that
Existing particleField uses effectiveCount = density × maxParticles × (0.6 + structureComplexity × 0.4) — plan's formula matches
ModeManager accepts ModeEntry[] with { name, factory } — plan's registration pattern is correct
FrameState has pointerX/pointerY as optional fields — pointer disturbance code must handle undefined
PRNG module uses mulberry32 — createPRNG(seed: string) returns () => number
20 tests is thorough and covers all acceptance criteria plus edge cases
